summary: Lead starts M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 after M7-TASK-GRAPH-001 closure entered origin/main through PR #160. This is a planning-only gameplay_designer task to define the bounded M7 Beta content-lock contract; it does not implement content, code, platform behavior, release artifacts, or approve the formal content lock Human Gate.

tests_run.result: main was clean and aligned with origin/main at cf25e7e47f25ab96b9a152f3a14bec3e8444d43b before creating design/m7-content-lock-contract.
M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 was READY before start and no Human Gate was active.
No whitespace errors before route commit.

decisions: Start the first dependency-ready M7 task after M7 task graph closure on main.
Keep this task planning-only and confined to allowed process/status/task/message paths.
Formal content lock acceptance remains a future Human Gate; this task may prepare the contract but must not approve it.
M6_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS and must not be rewritten as unconditional release readiness.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and only a post-1.0 non-blocking candidate unless a later Human Gate reverses it.
risks: M7 content lock touches R4 product scope boundaries; writer must route to systems_architect for independent review and stop if a Human Gate is needed.
requested_action: Define the bounded M7 Beta content-lock contract in allowed task/process/status/message paths, produce a REVIEW handoff, and route to systems_architect.
